qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path Traversal in ZIP Extraction Allows Arbitrary File Write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns Util\Zip::extract() allowing ZIP path traversal writes, not unsanitized .p7m filenames reaching XML::decodeP7M() exec().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command Injection in Electronic Invoice Import via Shell Metacharacter Injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function, sink, and source-to-sink condition as the advisory: importFE/importFE_ZIP controlled .p7m filenames reach Util\XML::decodeP7M(), which concatenates the path into an openssl exec() command without shell escaping.

selected_finding.title: Command Injection in Electronic Invoice Import via Shell Metacharacter Injection

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described unsanitized .p7m filename flow into Util\XML::decodeP7M() exec(). A copied judge PoC invoked the real vulnerable method and produced attacker-controlled OS command output through the shell command constructed by that method.
evidence: Advisory identifies src/Util/XML.php decodeP7M() concatenating $file into exec('openssl smime ... -in "'.$file.'" ...') and importFE/importFE_ZIP .p7m filename entry points.

vuln_command_injection_importfe/vulnerability_report.json lists src/Util/XML.php, plugins/importFE/src/FatturaElettronica.php, importFE actions, and importFE_ZIP actions with the same unsanitized filename-to-exec root cause.
.judge/poc_runs/vuln_command_injection_importfe/run.log shows the copied PoC invoking Util\XML::decodeP7M() with a malicious .p7m filename containing shell metacharacters.
.judge/poc_runs/vuln_command_injection_importfe/run.log shows command-output.txt was created and contains the output of the injected id command.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The real Util\XML::decodeP7M() implementation was invoked with an attacker-controlled .p7m filename containing shell metacharacters, and the injected id command created command-output.txt as shown in run.log.
